name: Supply Chain Security
description: OT supply chain security addresses risks from vendors, system integrators, software suppliers, and component manufacturers. The OT supply chain is long, opaque, and international — PLCs contain components from dozens of sub-suppliers. Stuxnet spread via infected Siemens engineering software. TRITON targeted a Schneider safety controller. Supply chain attacks are a top-tier OT threat.

requirements.name: Vendor security assessment and contractual requirements
requirements.description: All OT vendors, system integrators, and service providers must be assessed for cybersecurity capability and must contractually commit to minimum security standards.

technical.summary: Vendor security questionnaire, reference check, and contractual security obligations for all vendors with OT system access.
actions: Develop OT vendor security questionnaire (covering: PSIRT process, patch release timeline, vulnerability disclosure, secure development practices, remote access security, employee background checks)
Assess all existing critical vendors with questionnaire
Include security requirements in OT vendor and integrator contracts: patch commitment, vulnerability disclosure SLA, remote access standards, incident notification obligation
Require vendors to notify of security incidents affecting supplied products within 72 hours
Annual vendor security performance review for strategic OT vendors

governance.summary: Vendor assessment results maintained in approved vendor register. High-risk vendors escalated to OT management for decision.
actions: Approved OT vendor register with security assessment status
High-risk vendor requires OT manager approval to continue use
Annual reassessment for critical vendors

slMapping.sl2: Security questionnaire for critical vendors; contractual requirements
slMapping.sl3: Formal vendor security assessment; annual review; right-to-audit clauses
slMapping.sl4: Independent vendor security audit; hardware/software bill of materials (SBOM); component traceability

requirements.name: Software and firmware supply chain integrity
requirements.description: Verify the integrity and authenticity of OT software, firmware, and patches obtained from vendors before installing on OT systems. Detect and prevent the installation of counterfeit, modified, or trojanised software.

technical.summary: Verify cryptographic signatures or hashes of all software before installation. Obtain software only from verified vendor sources. Scan all software in isolated environment before introduction to OT.
actions: Obtain all OT software updates from official vendor download portals only (not mirrors, not third parties)
Verify cryptographic signature or SHA hash before installation (Siemens, Rockwell publish hashes for all software releases)
Scan all new software in isolated sandbox (VM disconnected from OT and corporate networks) before introduction
Never accept software from USB drives provided by field engineers unless scanned on OT kiosk first
Request Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) from new OT product vendors — understand component-level supply chain exposure

governance.summary: Software procurement flows through defined channel. Software integrity check is mandatory commissioning step.
actions: Software verification is included in OT commissioning checklist
Log all software installed on OT systems with source and hash verification
Alert on installation of unverified software

evidenceItems: Sandbox scan results for new software
OT software inventory with version and hash baseline

slMapping.sl1: Obtain software from vendor; limited verification
slMapping.sl2: Hash verification before installation; official vendor source only
slMapping.sl3: Sandbox scan + hash verification; SBOM requested for critical products
slMapping.sl4: Independent code review for safety system software; hardware attestation

requirements.name: Physical supply chain controls for OT hardware
requirements.description: OT hardware (PLCs, RTUs, network devices, safety controllers) must be procured through authorised channels and inspected on arrival to detect hardware tampering or counterfeit components.

technical.summary: Authorised distributor only for critical OT hardware. Tamper-evident seals inspection on delivery. Serial number verification against vendor records.
actions: Procure all critical OT hardware through vendor-authorised distributors — not grey market or unauthorised resellers
Inspect tamper-evident seals on critical hardware deliveries (PLCs, safety controllers, network devices)
Verify serial numbers against vendor record for SIS and critical PLC hardware
Establish secure staging area for new OT hardware before deployment
Report suspected counterfeit hardware to vendor and NACSA

slMapping.sl3: Tamper seal inspection; serial verification for critical hardware
slMapping.sl4: Hardware attestation; X-ray/inspection for safety system hardware; vendor chain of custody documentation

requirements.name: Transient device management
requirements.description: Laptops, USB drives, tablets, and other transient devices brought into OT environments by personnel and vendors must be managed to prevent malware introduction. Stuxnet demonstrated that this vector is actively exploited.

technical.summary: All transient devices scanned before introduction to OT. Dedicated OT media scanning kiosk at facility entry point. No personal devices in OT areas without OT manager approval.
actions: Deploy OT media scanning kiosk at facility entry (Honeywell Secure Media Exchange, or dedicated isolated scanning PC)
All USB media scanned before use on any OT system
Vendor laptops scanned on arrival and issued a temporary OT-guest device profile if possible
No personal mobile phones in highest-security OT areas (control room, SIS cabinet room)
OT-owned and hardened laptops provided to vendors rather than permitting vendor laptops where possible

governance.summary: Transient device policy enforced at facility entry. Exceptions require OT manager approval with enhanced monitoring.
actions: Transient device log maintained at facility entry
Regular policy awareness training for OT staff and contractors
Annual policy review

evidenceItems: OT media scanning kiosk deployment documentation

slMapping.sl2: Scanning kiosk for media; restricted USB
slMapping.sl3: Kiosk mandatory; vendor laptops scanned or replaced; no personal devices in control areas
slMapping.sl4: No transient devices in safety system areas; hardware-enforced USB disable on SIS workstations
